turns.content: Okay so -- I'm drowning. I'm working at like 110% and still falling behind, everything feels urgent, and I just -- what do I prioritize? Like where do I even start?
What's the feeling underneath "drowning" — is it more like fear, or more like exhaustion, or something else?

I mean -- it's just... there's so much to do? Like the feeling is that I have this big leadership thing that landed on my plate and there's a team meeting Thursday that I haven't prepped for, and my partner's been picking up the kids every day this week and I can feel that tension building, and -- yeah. It's just a lot. What do I cut first?

I notice you moved pretty quickly from the feeling to the list.

What's the one thing in all of that you're most scared to let drop?

The work thing. Definitely the work thing. Like if I drop the ball on Thursday -- I just took this role, right? Three months in. And I haven't fully -- I don't know, I haven't fully figured out how to do it yet. So if I blow the Thursday meeting it's... yeah. That one I can't drop.

But okay so given that, how do I protect Thursday? Like what do I actually cut to make space for it?
What happens if you blow Thursday?

I mean... it's bad. My boss is going to be there, the whole team, it's kind of a -- it's a visibility moment. So if I fumble it, people are going to see that I'm not -- 

I don't know. It's fine. I'll prep for it. I just need to figure out what else to move around so I have time. Can we just -- can we map out the week? Like what goes, what stays?

You just stopped yourself mid-sentence.

What were you about to say?

I just... I was going to say that people will see I'm not actually ready for this role. That I got promoted and I'm kind of -- faking it, maybe. But that's not -- I mean everyone feels that way, right? It's just imposter syndrome, it's not a real thing. I just need to prep better and manage my time and then Thursday will be fine.

So -- okay, the meeting. What do I actually need to have ready?

You named something real just now — "I'm not actually ready for this role" — and then you moved pretty fast to dismiss it.

What if it got to just sit there for a second, without being explained away?

*pause*

I mean... yeah. Okay. It's... 

*longer pause*

It's there. It's kind of always there, actually. Under all of -- yeah. I don't really let myself think about it because if I stop moving long enough to think about it then I have to actually... feel it. And I have a call at two so.

*laughs a little*

Sorry. That's -- I don't know where that came from.

Don't apologize. That came from somewhere true.

What does it feel like, right now, to just let it be there?

It's... uncomfortable. Like I want to immediately make a plan or write something down or -- 

*quiet*

It's kind of heavy, actually. I didn't realize how heavy it was. I've just been going so fast that I didn't -- 

I keep thinking if I just get through this month, if I just nail Thursday, if I just get on top of the list, then I'll feel like I can do this job. But that's... that's been the plan for three months and the list just keeps getting longer.

Yeah.

What if getting on top of the list isn't actually what would make you feel ready?

*sits with that*

Then I... huh.

Then I don't know what would. Which is -- okay that's kind of a terrifying thought actually. Because the list is the thing I know how to do. Like I'm good at the list. I can manage the list. But if the list isn't the answer then...

*trails off*

What if I'm just -- what if I'm actually not ready and no amount of prepping Thursday fixes that? What if my boss figures that out?
